commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5563b34e591d2e9a9f7f4ae408df3aca77ad42ee)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a memory-core runtime path that rewrites a stored FTS-only sentinel providerKey to the current providerKey during identity refresh, plus regression tests and a test-project snapshot entry.

PR surface: Source +37, Tests +244. Total +281 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. Current source initializes the provider before CLI sync writes metadata, the proposed prerequisite PR is closed unmerged, and the PR's positive test simulates the state by editing SQLite directly.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the blocker is proof and fix direction for persisted memory identity, not a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: [AI] fix(memory): auto-fix providerKey mismatch from CLI index --force
target.body:
## Summary

Running `openclaw memory index --force` writes a `providerKey` based on the initialized provider's `cacheKeyData` (which may include `baseUrl`, `headers`, `dimensions`). When the runtime manager later initializes with different `cacheKeyData` (e.g. the CLI lacked runtime context), the `providerKey` comparison fails and `memory_search` is permanently disabled with "index provider settings changed" until meta is manually rewritten.

Now `refreshIndexIdentityDirty` auto-fixes the **stale FTS-only sentinel** providerKey only: when the stored `meta.providerKey` is the exact FTS-only sentinel `SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})` that CLI writes before provider initialization, and the runtime has since resolved a real provider, the stale key is updated to the current computed `providerKey`. Genuine provider-setting changes (different dimensions, baseUrl, headers) still require reindexing — the auto-fix predicate only matches the known stale CLI identity.

- Problem: CLI `openclaw memory index --force` writes stale `providerKey` `SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})` before provider init, causing runtime `memory_search` to be permanently disabled with "index provider settings changed"
- Solution: In `refreshIndexIdentityDirty`, when provider is initialized and `meta.providerKey` is the exact FTS-only sentinel, update it to the current `providerKey`. This preserves the reindex safeguard for genuine same-provider/model setting changes.
- What changed: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` (narrowed auto-fix predicate + `hashText` import), `src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` (snapshot), test file `manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts` (4 tests)
- What did NOT change: Identity check logic in `resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState`, CLI code, config surfaces, default values

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue

- Refs #91902 (providerKey mismatch auto-fix for stale FTS-only sentinel; the scopeHash issue mentioned in the issue is separate and not addressed here)

## Motivation

Issue #91902 reports that `openclaw memory index --force` disables `memory_search` with "index built with a different provider". Root cause: CLI creates `MemoryIndexManager` with `purpose="cli"` and no `providerResult`, so `computeProviderKey()` returns `SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})` before provider initialization. After `ensureProviderInitialized()` runs during sync, the `providerKey` is recomputed with the real provider's `cacheKeyData` — but this new key is written into meta. When a runtime manager later starts, the `providerKey` comparison fails and search is permanently disabled.

The fix is narrow and fail-closed: only the known stale FTS-only sentinel is auto-repaired. Genuine `cacheKeyData` differences (dimensions, baseUrl, headers) still trigger reindexing.

## Changes

**File**: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts`

In `refreshIndexIdentityDirty`, after computing the identity state:
- If state is "mismatched" with reason "index provider settings changed", provider is initialized, and `meta.providerKey` equals the FTS-only sentinel `SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})`
- Update `meta.providerKey` to `this.providerKey` and write back
- Re-evaluate identity state with the updated meta
- **Does NOT auto-fix** when `meta.providerKey` is any other value (e.g. a different `cacheKeyData`-derived hash from changed dimensions/baseUrl/headers)

Added `hashText` import and lazy static `getFtsOnlyProviderKey()` to compute the sentinel.

**File**: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts` (new)

4 tests:
1. Auto-fixes stale FTS-only providerKey and allows search to proceed
2. Does not auto-fix when provider id also differs (genuine mismatched)
3. Does not auto-fix when runtime dimensions differ from index
4. Does not auto-fix when runtime baseUrl differs from index

**File**: `src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`

Added `manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts` to the memory-core test file snapshot.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `memory_search` is permanently disabled with "index provider settings changed" after CLI `openclaw memory index --force` writes the FTS-only sentinel `providerKey` before provider initialization
- Real environment tested: Linux (RHEL), Node.js 22.22, local checkout on branch `fix/memory-providerkey-mismatch-91902` at commit `be3d9241`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
# 1. Run the providerkey auto-fix tests (positive + negative)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

# 2. Run the test-projects snapshot test
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts --run

# 3. Run existing regression tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.self-heal-missing-identity.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts --run
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.providerkey-auto-fix.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000363/requre-an/MAAS-MR/memory-work-main/07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw

 ✓ auto-fixes stale FTS-only providerKey written by CLI before provider init 186ms
 ✓ does not auto-fix when provider id also differs 75ms
 ✓ does not auto-fix when runtime dimensions differ from index 81ms
 ✓ does not auto-fix when runtime baseUrl differs from index 102ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts --run

 ✓ |tooling| src/scripts/test-projects.test.ts (82 tests) 9697ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  82 passed (82)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.self-heal-missing-identity.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts --run

 ✓ manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts (7 tests) 647ms
 ✓ manager.self-heal-missing-identity.test.ts (2 tests) 173ms
 ✓ manager-reindex-state.test.ts (9 tests) 23ms
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
```

- Observed result after fix: When `meta.providerKey` is the exact FTS-only sentinel `SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})`, `refreshIndexIdentityDirty` updates it to the current computed `providerKey` and re-evaluates identity as "valid". Search proceeds normally. When `meta.providerKey` is any other value (different dimensions, different baseUrl, different provider id), the auto-fix does NOT apply and search remains blocked — preserving the reindex safeguard for genuine provider-setting changes.
- What was not tested: Live CLI `openclaw memory index --force` followed by runtime `memory_search` with a real openai-compatible provider. The stale FTS-only providerKey is simulated by writing it into meta after a valid sync.

## Risks

- **Narrow predicate**: The auto-fix ONLY activates when `meta.providerKey === SHA256({"provider":"none","model":"fts-only"})`, the exact sentinel CLI writes before provider init. Any other `providerKey` value (from different dimensions, baseUrl, headers, or genuine setting changes) is NOT auto-fixed and still requires reindexing.
- **Fail-closed**: If the predicate does not match, the existing mismatched behavior is preserved unchanged.
- **Additive**: The change only adds a repair path for one known stale identity; it does not change the identity check logic in `resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState`.
- **No config/default surface changes**: No new config keys, env vars, or defaults introduced.
- **Scope limitation**: The `scopeHash` mismatch issue also mentioned in #91902 is not addressed here — it requires a separate fix for `normalizeMemoryMultimodalSettings` default handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who previously saw "memory search is paused because the memory index was built with a different embedding provider/model/settings" after running `openclaw memory index --force` (where the meta contains the stale FTS-only sentinel providerKey) will now have search automatically restored on the next runtime sync or search. Users who changed actual provider settings (dimensions, baseUrl, etc.) will still see the mismatched warning and need to reindex.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Stale FTS-only providerKey auto-fix (test 1), provider id mismatch no-fix (test 2), dimensions differ no-fix (test 3), baseUrl differ no-fix (test 4), existing self-heal and reindex-state tests still pass, test-projects snapshot updated
- Edge cases checked: Provider not initialized (auto-fix skipped), provider id differs (auto-fix skipped), runtime dimensions differ (auto-fix skipped), runtime baseUrl differs (auto-fix skipped)
- What you did NOT verify: Live CLI + runtime end-to-end with real provider

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #91902 analysis → source code exploration → initial broad auto-fix → ClawSweeper review feedback → narrowed predicate to stale FTS-only sentinel only + negative tests for dimensions/baseUrl/provider-id changes

---

Refs #91902
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
